question: During what years did the railroad that had a station stop in Ringling, Montana operate? Write your answer as [ANSWER].

1.fact: Ringling, Montana: The town was a station stop on the transcontinental main line of the Chicago, Milwaukee, St. Paul and Pacific Railroad ("the Milwaukee Road"); it was also the southern terminus of the White Sulphur Springs and Yellowstone Park Railway, which ran from Ringling to White Sulphur Springs.
1.subquestion: Which railroad had a station stop in Ringling, Montana?
1.answer: Chicago, Milwaukee, St. Paul and Pacific Railroad

2.source: other-benchmarks/raw/hotpotqa/hotpot_dev_distractor_v1.json#5a86eee85542994775f60787:Chicago, Milwaukee, St. Paul and Pacific Railroad
2.fact: Chicago, Milwaukee, St. Paul and Pacific Railroad: The Chicago, Milwaukee, St. Paul and Pacific Railroad (often referred to as the Milwaukee Road) (reporting mark MILW) , was a Class I railroad that operated in the Midwest and Northwest of the United States from 1847 until 1980, when its Pacific Extension (Montana, Idaho, and Washington) was abandoned following a bankruptcy.
2.subquestion: During what years did the Chicago, Milwaukee, St. Paul and Pacific Railroad operate?
